scope: add cartservice (.NET, nested context src/cartservice/src) to gmd-app source-build PoC; no contract flip, no routing, no chart change, no adservice work

nested_context_dispatch.rationale: Mirrors upstream skaffold.yaml convention; the existing per-service map already represents non-flat contexts so no script structural change was required.

updated_existing_tests: test_gmd_app_build_supported_services_tuple now pins all ten entries with cartservice last
test_gmd_app_build_command_still_rejects_known_deferred_services no longer asserts cartservice (now supported); still asserts adservice and shoppingassistantservice
test_gmd_app_build_command_rejects_unsupported_service updated to use adservice (Java) as the negative example since cartservice is now supported

commits_landed_to_canonical_main.summary: feat(gmd-app): add cartservice (.NET, nested context) to source-build PoC

what_remains_before_chart_integration_li.summary: Only adservice remains as a chart-default first-party blocker. Once it lands in the dispatch table, the script can produce all 11 chart-default first-party gmd-deployable images.

for_remaining_one_service.adservice: Java toolchain (Gradle/JDK distroless). Adds a new substrate base set (e.g. eclipse-temurin or equivalent JDK + JRE) to whatever cluster-builder mirror is later chosen. Host-only host docker has the bandwidth to pull these from Docker Hub today; cluster-builder integration would require mirroring.

for_chart_integration_minimal.approach: Recommended Option B from UP8-source-boundary audit: add helm.parameters to the dev element of infrastructure/gitops/gmd/applicationset.yaml flipping `images.repository` -> `host.k3d.internal:5000/gmd` and `images.tag` -> the gmd-app source-build tag for `gmd-dev` only.
for_chart_integration_minimal.blocked_until: all 11 chart-default first-party services build successfully on the chosen substrate. After UP8-18, 10/11 are covered; only adservice remains.

for_lifecycle_dispatch_wiring.change_required: scripts/amof/api/routers/release.py release_lifecycle_action() must branch on name == 'gmd' so action == 'build' calls build_gmd_app_lifecycle_build_command instead of build_lifecycle_build_command. Probably needs a thin wrapper that iterates the dispatch table in one lifecycle invocation.
for_lifecycle_dispatch_wiring.blocked_until: the command can produce the full set of chart-default gmd-deployable images, otherwise dispatched builds would always be incomplete

for_contract_flip.change_required: _ecosystem_build_contract('gmd') -> source_build with produces / source_repos / build_command metadata; update lifecycle contract test that pins kind == 'mirror_upstream'
for_contract_flip.blocked_until: lifecycle dispatch wiring is in place AND the chart actually consumes the locally-built images

cluster_builder_substrate_decision.open_question: Does the gmd lifecycle build need to run inside the in-cluster builder pod (same path as AMOF platform substrate)?
cluster_builder_substrate_decision.if_yes_required_work_after_full_service_: Mirror per-language base images so the builder pod's containerd can pull them: golang:1.26.1-alpine, gcr.io/distroless/static, python:3.14.3-alpine, node:20.20.1-alpine, alpine:3.23.3, mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/sdk:10.0.100-noble, mcr.microsoft.com/dotnet/runtime-deps:10.0.0-noble-chiseled, plus the Java base for adservice once it lands.
cluster_builder_substrate_decision.if_no: Host-only builds suffice for local development; production / cloud-dev substrate remains a separate decision.

what_to_implement: Add adservice (Java) to scripts/build-gmd-app.sh dispatch table.
Update _GMD_APP_BUILD_SUPPORTED_SERVICES (eleventh entry) and add a focused test.
Run host build for adservice and verify registry proof (this is the Java toolchain pull on the host docker).
After adservice lands, the prerequisite for chart integration / lifecycle dispatch wiring / contract flip is satisfied for all chart-default first-party services.
